$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/config.text:
\define edit(function) <$edit-text index="key-$function$" tag="input" size="10"/> <$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings"> !! Shortcuts As this plugin is very keyboard driven, it's important to have this setup to your liking. Some notes in particular: * The default ordered shortcut (ctrl-3) is what TW defaults to for header level 3, so either change the default, or change below * If you're using edit-comptext, then the up, down, and enter keys need to be addressed before they can work together ** For example changing "add-sibling" from `enter` to `shift-enter` allows you to still auto-select the first entry |input|function|single|family|h |keyboard
[ext[help|https://tiddlywiki.com/#KeyboardWidget]]|select-up|<>|-| |~|select-down|<>|-| |~|add-sibling|<>|-| |~|cancel-edit-line|<>|-| |~|delete-line|<>|<>| |~|promote|<> or <>|<>| |~|demote|<> or <>|<>| |~|move-up|<>|<>| |~|move-down|<>|<>| |~|ordered (#)|<>|<>| |~|unordered (*)|<>|<>| |~|save all edits|<>|<| |mouse
[ext[help|https://tiddlywiki.com/#modifier%20Variable]]|select|click|-| |~|reparent|<>-click|<>-click| !! Formatting (css) By default the bullet editing mode is meant to look as close as possible to normal viewing mode. You might prefer that instead it look more unique during the editing experience. A class is used around the viewer called `bullets-viewer`. Below is a shortcut on changing the whole of the viewing area, as well as formatting specific to the actual lines. Viewer:
<$edit-text tiddler="$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings" index="css-viewer" tag="input" size="40"/>
For example, to put a red border around the editing area, try `border: 1px solid red;` Lines:
<$edit-text tiddler="$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings" index="css-listitem" tag="input" size="40"/>
For example, to make your list lines blue, try `color: blue;` !! Link Fixer There is a built-in link-fixing capability while editing bullets. You can turn this feature off, for example if you'd rather use the edit-comptext plugin. You can also tweak the number of results for each link to be fixed. Enabled: (default:on)
<$checkbox tiddler="$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings" index="link-fixer" checked="on" unchecked="off"> Enable link fixer Results: (default:2)
<$edit-text tiddler="$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings" index="link-fixer-results" tag="input" size="2"/>
$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/config.title: $:/plugins/stobot/bullets/config

$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/editor.text:
\define linkfix-actions() <$action-setfield $tiddler="$(currentTiddler)$" $value={{{ [[$(currentTiddler)$]get[text]search-replace:regexp[$(links)$],[$(matches)$]] }}}/> <$edit field="text" tag="input" focus="true" class="bullets-editor" refreshTitle=<> ><$set name="targetTiddler" value=<> ><$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[$:/tags/EditorToolbar]!has[draft.of]]" ><$reveal type="nomatch" state=<> text="hide" class="tc-text-editor-toolbar-item-wrapper hide-buttons" ><$transclude tiddler="$:/core/ui/EditTemplate/body/toolbar/button" mode="inline" /><$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[$:/tags/EditorTools]!has[draft.of]]" ><$list filter={{!!condition}} variable="list-condition" ><$transclude/> <$list filter="[[$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings]getindex[link-fixer]match[on]]" variable="x"> <$list filter="[links[]]" variable="links"> <$list filter="[!is[tiddler]]" variable="x">
Replace [[<>]] with: <$list filter="[search:title] :sort:number[length[]] +[first{$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings##link-fixer-results}]" variable="matches"> <$button class="tc-btn-invisible tc-tiddlylink" actions=<>>[[<$text text=<>/>]]

$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/macros.title: $:/plugins/stobot/bullets/macros

$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/readme.text:
!!! Bullets plugin

This plugin makes the list editing experience a little closer to [ext[WYSIWYG|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WYSIWYG]] like other knowledge management programs I use like ~OneNote and ~FreeMind/~FreePlane. Eventually I'll have at least 3 plugins in this series, one being [ext[Stickies|https://stobot.github.io/sticky/]] which allows you to flag text like ~OneNote's flag for later, and the other will be ''Tables'' which will edit wikitext tables like ~OneNote as well. 

For a demonstration of all of the features of ''Bullets'' as well as future updates, refer to its home page https://stobot.github.io/bullets/
$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/readme.title: $:/plugins/stobot/bullets/readme

$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/stylesheet.title: $:/plugins/stobot/bullets/stylesheet

$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings.text:
key-up: up
key-down: down
key-add: enter
key-delete: ctrl-delete
key-delete-family: shift-ctrl-delete
key-cancel: escape
key-promote: shift-alt-left
key-promote-family: ctrl-alt-left
key-promote2: shift+tab
key-demote: shift-alt-right
key-demote-family: ctrl-alt-right
key-demote2: tab
key-moveup: shift-alt-up
key-moveup-family: ctrl-alt-up
key-movedown: shift-alt-down
key-movedown-family: ctrl-alt-down
key-ordered: ctrl-3
key-ordered-family: shift-ctrl-3
key-unordered: ctrl-8
key-unordered-family: shift-ctrl-8
key-select: 
key-reparent: ctrl
key-reparent-family: ctrl-shift
key-save: ctrl-enter
css-viewer: 
css-listitem: 
link-fixer: on
link-fixer-results: 2
$:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings.title: $:/plugins/stobot/bullets/settings
